req.title: KYC refresh (negative variant)
req.statement: A customer shall complete a KYC refresh every 365 days; accounts beyond refresh window shall be restricted to inbound transactions only. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-065 mandates KYC refresh every 365 days with account restriction beyond that window. The test suite covers: (1) a positive EP case where a fully compliant submission within the refresh window succeeds and sets customer.verified=true; (2) a negative NEG case where a submission fails compliance checks and returns HTTP 403; (3) a BVA boundary case at exactly day 365 (the last valid day) to confirm the account is not yet restricted; (4) a BVA boundary case at day 366 (first day beyond window) to confirm inbound-only restriction is enforced; (5) a STC state-transition case confirming a restricted account transitions to verified after successful refresh; (6) a DT case combining unauthorised token with refresh attempt to confirm 403 is returned regardless of payload validity. This covers all specified negative paths (insufficient-auth, compliance failure, limit exceeded) and the 365-day window boundary.

tests.name: test_kyc_refresh_valid_submission_within_window
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid bearer token
Customer last completed KYC fewer than 365 days ago
All submitted KYC documents pass compliance rules
actions: POST /kyc/refresh with a well-formed payload and valid auth header
Assert response body contains a refreshed timestamp
trace: R-FIN-065: successful KYC refresh sets customer.verified=true
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from datetime import datetime, timezone

BASE_URL = "<base_url>"

@pytest.fixture
def valid_auth_headers():
    return <fixture_valid_auth_headers>

@pytest.fixture
def kyc_payload_valid():
    return <fixture_kyc_payload_valid>

def test_kyc_refresh_valid_submission_within_window(valid_auth_headers, kyc_payload_valid):
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{BASE_URL}/kyc/refresh",
        json=kyc_payload_valid,
        headers=valid_auth_headers,
        timeout=10
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200, f"Expected 200, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("customer", {}).get("verified") is True
    assert "kyc_refreshed_at" in body
    refreshed_at = datetime.fromisoformat(body["kyc_refreshed_at"])
    assert (datetime.now(timezone.utc) - refreshed_at).total_seconds() < 60

tests.name: test_kyc_refresh_compliance_failure_returns_403
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid bearer token
Submitted KYC payload contains data that will fail compliance checks (e.g. blacklisted document)
actions: POST /kyc/refresh with a payload engineered to fail compliance
Assert response body contains an error code indicating compliance failure
expected: Response body error_code indicates compliance rejection
trace: R-FIN-065: error pathway: 403 when submission fails compliance
R-FIN-065: negative paths must be tested

tests.name: test_kyc_refresh_on_day_365_still_accepted
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid bearer token
Customer last completed KYC exactly 365 days ago (last valid day of window)
actions: Seed account with kyc_last_refreshed_at = today - 365 days via <helper_fn_seed_kyc_date>
expected: HTTP 200 OK — day 365 is still within the allowed refresh window
trace: R-FIN-065: accounts beyond refresh window restricted; day 365 is the boundary ON value
BVA: ON boundary — last valid day must still succeed

tests.name: test_kyc_refresh_on_day_366_account_restricted_inbound_only
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid bearer token
Customer last completed KYC exactly 366 days ago (first day beyond window)
Account is expected to be in restricted state
actions: Seed account with kyc_last_refreshed_at = today - 366 days via <helper_fn_seed_kyc_date>
expected: Account status shows restricted=true and inbound_only=true
trace: R-FIN-065: accounts beyond refresh window restricted to inbound transactions only
BVA: OFF boundary — day 366 must trigger restriction
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from datetime import date, timedelta

BASE_URL = "<base_url>"

@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def seed_kyc_day_366():
    last_refresh = (date.today() - timedelta(days=366)).isoformat()
    <helper_fn_seed_kyc_date>(customer_id="<test_customer_id>", last_refresh=last_refresh)
    yield
    <helper_fn_reset_kyc_date>(customer_id="<test_customer_id>")

@pytest.fixture
def valid_auth_headers():
    return <fixture_valid_auth_headers>

def test_kyc_refresh_on_day_366_account_restricted_inbound_only(valid_auth_headers):
    status_resp = requests.get(
        f"{BASE_URL}/account/status",
        headers=valid_auth_headers,
        timeout=10
    )
    assert status_resp.status_code == 200
    status = status_resp.json()
    assert status.get("restricted") is True
    assert status.get("inbound_only") is True
    outbound_resp = requests.post(
        f"{BASE_URL}/transactions/outbound",
        json=<fixture_outbound_txn_payload>,
        headers=valid_auth_headers,
        timeout=10
    )
    assert outbound_resp.status_code == 403

tests.name: test_kyc_refresh_transitions_restricted_account_to_verified
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid bearer token
Account is currently in RESTRICTED state (KYC overdue)
actions: Seed account into RESTRICTED KYC state via <helper_fn_seed_restricted_kyc>
Confirm account is restricted via GET /account/status
Confirm account transitions to VERIFIED via GET /account/status
expected: Post-condition: account.status is VERIFIED and restricted=false
trace: R-FIN-065: successful refresh expected outcome: customer.verified=true
STC: RESTRICTED -> VERIFIED state transition on successful KYC refresh
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "<base_url>"

@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def seed_restricted_account():
    <helper_fn_seed_restricted_kyc>(customer_id="<test_customer_id>")
    yield
    <helper_fn_reset_kyc_date>(customer_id="<test_customer_id>")

@pytest.fixture
def valid_auth_headers():
    return <fixture_valid_auth_headers>

@pytest.fixture
def kyc_payload_valid():
    return <fixture_kyc_payload_valid>

def test_kyc_refresh_transitions_restricted_account_to_verified(valid_auth_headers, kyc_payload_valid):
    pre = requests.get(f"{BASE_URL}/account/status", headers=valid_auth_headers, timeout=10)
    assert pre.json().get("restricted") is True, "Precondition: account must be restricted"
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{BASE_URL}/kyc/refresh",
        json=kyc_payload_valid,
        headers=valid_auth_headers,
        timeout=10
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    post = requests.get(f"{BASE_URL}/account/status", headers=valid_auth_headers, timeout=10)
    post_body = post.json()
    assert post_body.get("restricted") is False
    assert post_body.get("customer", {}).get("verified") is True

tests.name: test_kyc_refresh_unauthorised_token_returns_403
preconditions: An invalid or expired bearer token is used
KYC payload is otherwise well-formed and compliant
actions: POST /kyc/refresh with an expired/invalid bearer token and valid payload
Assert response body contains an authentication error code
expected: HTTP 401 or 403 — unauthorised access must be rejected regardless of payload validity
Response body contains error_code UNAUTHORISED or FORBIDDEN
trace: R-FIN-065: negative paths: unauthorised must be tested
DT: rule — invalid auth token x valid payload = 403/401 rejection
